items.front: What are the basic ways to establish longitudinal separation procedurally?
items.back: Use specified times for departure, arrival at a fix, holding at a fix, or altitude changes at a stated time or fix.

items.front: What speed advantage supports the basic same, converging, or crossing-course longitudinal minimum?
items.back: The leading aircraft must maintain at least a 44-knot speed advantage over the following aircraft for the listed 5-mile or 3-minute minimum.

items.front: How is opposite-course longitudinal separation protected before aircraft pass each other?
items.back: Assign approved vertical separation from 10 minutes before until 10 minutes after the estimated pass, then discontinue it only after one of the paragraph's passing conditions is met.

items.front: When may pilots maintain longitudinal separation from each other on the same course?
items.back: When the pilots are in direct radio communication, concur, and the following aircraft is authorized to maintain 10 minutes or 20 miles from the leading aircraft.

items.front: What must be told to an RNAV aircraft when DME separation is applied along a VOR airway or route?
items.back: Advise the pilot to use DME distances.
